title: The embryos label
wikitext:
[]: The embryo's label             Quote Post by Anti-Goth » Fri Jan 25, 2008 8:50 pm I don't know if anyone else has thought about this much. When I was checking out the wiki, I took another look at the picture of embryo-Adam. Check out the text, I first noticed this in the wiki too: Whoisyou Heis Living Why? Overanalysis, I know. But something tells me this text is significant. My first impression was that 'whoisyou' applied to Gendo, while 'heis living why?' referred to Adam still being alive, as Kaji said. However, Adam is generally agreed as being female. And who is the 'who' in 'whoisyou'? Thoughts? There's something bigger here, if you ask me...

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Fri Jan 25, 2008 8:54 pm Wow, great find!

[]:             Quote Post by Anti-Goth » Fri Jan 25, 2008 8:58 pm Sachiel_13 wrote: Wow, great find!

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Fri Jan 25, 2008 9:04 pm Actually, it looks more like "Whoissyoui".

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Fri Jan 25, 2008 9:10 pm "Whoisyour" is also possible, now that you mentioned it. "Whoisyour Heisliving Why?" If the "Your" is for "Your vessel", then it might be asking "Why is Kaworu living?" I can't make any further sense of it, and I've got no idea why that was included.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Fri Jan 25, 2008 9:28 pm @ Anti-Goth [Darth Vader voice] The fanwankery is STRONG with this one! [/Darth Vader voice]

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Fri Jan 25, 2008 10:05 pm The "He is living why?" actually may be something. Whoever wrote it was probably wondering why Adam was still alive. Then again, the soul wasn't actually in that body, was it? Still, it's there and it's Engrishy enough to be open to interpretation. Though this is different in that we can actually read it, this kind of reminds me of L IS REAL 2401.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Fri Jan 25, 2008 10:11 pm Why does everyone overlook the label of "sample" on it? If that's only a sample... where's the rest?

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Fri Jan 25, 2008 10:14 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: Why does everyone overlook the label of "sample" on it? If that's only a sample... where's the rest?

[]: Re: The embryo's label             Quote Post by V » Fri Jan 25, 2008 10:37 pm Anti-Goth wrote: I don't know if anyone else has thought about this much. When I was checking out the wiki, I took another look at the picture of embryo-Adam.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Fri Jan 25, 2008 11:46 pm Sachiel_13 wrote: Perhaps the rest is with Kaworu?

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sat Jan 26, 2008 12:03 am Eva Yojimbo wrote: So again, it makes me wonder where they got that sample from.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sat Jan 26, 2008 12:22 am Anonymous_Evafan wrote: The South Poll?

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sat Jan 26, 2008 12:25 am Eva Yojimbo wrote: even though he knew about the embryo being in Gendo

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sat Jan 26, 2008 12:31 am Sachiel_13 wrote: He did?

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sat Jan 26, 2008 12:35 am Eva Yojimbo wrote: Sachiel_13 wrote: He did?

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sat Jan 26, 2008 12:47 am DC of ep. 24', yes.

[]:             Quote Post by Skullraper » Sat Jan 26, 2008 1:37 am Heis Living Why? To me, it reads like; “how are you alive?” It could be referring to the fact that the sample is a living organism despite the absence of it’s soul. The question could be asking; “how are you alive without a soul?” The same could be asked of Lilith. I have a theory but would rather not elaborate at the moment. Heis Living reads like a response to Whoisyou . It’s probably meant to be general. Sample is defined as a piece or limited quantity of material, usually from a larger amount, taken or provided for testing, analysis, inspection, demonstration, or trial use. The embryo is referred to as a sample because it is literally a sample of Adam used for the construction of the evas. I don’t believe there’s anything elaborate about the label.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Jan 26, 2008 3:31 am The usual transcription is "Who is a man? he is crying. why?"

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sat Jan 26, 2008 5:53 am Eva Yojimbo wrote: The label of sample seems confusing as it implies there's a rest somewhere, so why is that all we ever see of it?
